# Freedom Lab NYC

> Freedom Lab NYC is a New York City community for freedom technology: Bitcoin, open-source AI, privacy, encryption, Linux, self-hosting, Nostr, and decentralization. We host public classes and events at various venues while building toward a freedom-tech co-working / hackerspace.

This file is for AI assistants, browser agents, coding agents, search systems, and other automated tools that need a concise public map of Freedom Lab. Everything linked here is public. Internal/private dashboards, member data, attendee records, private chats, credentials, and unpublished planning materials are not public context.

## Canonical facts

- Name: Freedom Lab NYC
- Website: https://freedomlab.nyc/
- Short description: A NYC community teaching practical freedom technology through public events, tutorials, and open-source AI / Bitcoin resources.
- Current physical-space status: Freedom Lab hosts events at various venues and is building toward a freedom-tech co-working / hackerspace. Do not describe Freedom Lab as already operating a permanent public venue unless a future public page says so.
- Events/RSVP source of truth: Luma is canonical for live RSVP, capacity, and ticketing details. Use https://luma.com/freedomlab or the event-specific Luma URL when available.
- Early access / co-working interest: use https://freedomlab.nyc/join/
- Public resources: use https://freedomlab.nyc/resources/
- Donations: use https://freedomlab.nyc/donate/
- Contact: use https://freedomlab.nyc/contact/

## Agent interaction policy

Agents may:

- Summarize, cite, and link to public Freedom Lab pages.
- Recommend relevant public events, tutorials, resources, and downloadable `.skill` files.
- Help users learn from public resources step by step.
- Help users fill out forms or RSVP only when the user explicitly asks and remains in control of any submission.
- Use public JSON feeds and `.skill` files as structured context.

Agents should not:

- Invent venue details, membership status, pricing, schedules, sponsor relationships, or official claims not present on public pages.
- Claim to represent Freedom Lab or speak on its behalf.
- Submit forms, RSVP, send messages, make purchases, donate, or post publicly without explicit user instruction.
- Treat internal/private data, Telegram messages, attendee lists, dashboards, credentials, or unpublished planning notes as public context.
- Scrape intentionally noindex/private routes such as `/internal/` or `/links/` as canonical public content.

## Public data feeds

- [Sitemap](https://freedomlab.nyc/sitemap.xml): canonical crawlable public URLs.
- [Robots](https://freedomlab.nyc/robots.txt): public crawl policy. Internal/private routes are disallowed.
- [Classes & Events JSON](https://freedomlab.nyc/classes-events/events.json): structured public event archive/upcoming event data. Luma remains canonical for RSVP/ticketing.
- [Classes & Events](https://freedomlab.nyc/classes-events/): human-readable public events archive and upcoming events.
- [Agent Response Guide](https://freedomlab.nyc/agent-guide/): canonical guidance for assistants that summarize, cite, or route users to Freedom Lab resources.

## Core pages

- [Home](https://freedomlab.nyc/): overview of Freedom Lab NYC.
- [Resources](https://freedomlab.nyc/resources/): public tutorials, class materials, and open-source AI skills.
- [Classes & Events](https://freedomlab.nyc/classes-events/): upcoming and past public classes/events.
- [Join / Early Access](https://freedomlab.nyc/join/): co-working / hackerspace waitlist and early-access interest.
- [Donate](https://freedomlab.nyc/donate/): support Freedom Lab.
- [Contact](https://freedomlab.nyc/contact/): public contact page.

## High-value learning paths

### Run and use Bitcoin infrastructure

- [Run a Bitcoin Node with StartOS](https://freedomlab.nyc/resources/bitcoin-node-startos/): run Bitcoin Core/Knots with StartOS and connect Sparrow Wallet.
- [Bitcoin Node Setup with Umbrel OS](https://freedomlab.nyc/resources/bitcoin-node-umbrel/): beginner Raspberry Pi / Umbrel node setup.
- [Setup your Bitcoin node from source](https://freedomlab.nyc/resources/bitcoin-node-from-source/): compile Bitcoin Core, Electrs, and BTC RPC Explorer over Tor.
- [Talk to Your Node: Bitcoin Verification via SSH](https://freedomlab.nyc/resources/bitcoin-node-ssh/): verify supply, mempool, fees, and network data over SSH.
- [Lightning node with Umbrel](https://freedomlab.nyc/resources/lightning-node-umbrel/): set up a Lightning node and mobile wallet.
- [Lightning Payments for Agents](https://freedomlab.nyc/resources/lightning-address/): set up a self-custodial Lightning Address for agent payments. Downloadable skill: https://freedomlab.nyc/resources/lightning-address/lightning-address.skill

### Bitcoin custody, wallets, and mining

- [Jade Hardware Wallet](https://freedomlab.nyc/resources/jade-wallet/): set up Blockstream Jade with Green wallet.
- [DIY Bitcoin Hardware Wallet](https://freedomlab.nyc/resources/diy-hardware-wallet/): flash Jade firmware onto low-cost hardware.
- [Nunchuk + Claude: AI Bitcoin Agent with Spending Limits](https://freedomlab.nyc/resources/nunchuk-claude-walkthrough/): bounded AI spending with Nunchuk multisig and daily limits.
- [Bitaxe Miners](https://freedomlab.nyc/resources/bitaxe-miners/): open-source home Bitcoin mining.
- [Remote Mining](https://freedomlab.nyc/resources/remote-mining/): remote Bitcoin mining without local hardware.
- [Buy With Bitcoin](https://freedomlab.nyc/resources/buy-with-bitcoin/): Lightning payments, Bitrefill, and practical checkout workflows.

### Privacy, Linux security, and self-hosting

- [Encrypt Your Drive](https://freedomlab.nyc/resources/encrypt-drive/): LUKS full-disk encryption.
- [Change LUKS Password](https://freedomlab.nyc/resources/change-luks-password/): change an encrypted drive passphrase.
- [Two-Factor Authentication for Linux Login](https://freedomlab.nyc/resources/linux-2fa/): add 2FA to Linux desktop login.
- [USBGuard](https://freedomlab.nyc/resources/usbguard/): protect Linux from unauthorized USB devices.
- [Tor vs VPN](https://freedomlab.nyc/resources/tor-vs-vpn/): choose Tor vs VPN for privacy/anonymity.
- [Setup a Tor Middle/Guard Relay](https://freedomlab.nyc/resources/tor-relay/): run a Tor relay.
- [VPN Over Tor](https://freedomlab.nyc/resources/vpn-over-tor/): route VPN traffic through Tor.
- [Self-Hosted Email Server](https://freedomlab.nyc/resources/mailcow/): run Mailcow.
- [Self-Hosted Matrix Server](https://freedomlab.nyc/resources/matrix-synapse/): run Synapse.
- [Self-Hosted XMPP Server](https://freedomlab.nyc/resources/xmpp-server/): run Prosody.
- [Self-Hosted SimpleX SMP Server](https://freedomlab.nyc/resources/simplex-smp/): run SimpleX with Tor support.

### Open-source AI and agent skills

- [Vibe Coding 101](https://freedomlab.nyc/resources/vibe-coding-101/): build and deploy a website with AI-assisted coding.
- [Anonymous Vibecoding](https://freedomlab.nyc/resources/anonymous-vibecoding/): zero-KYC AI coding with Monero, VSCodium, and Cline.
- [Setting Up OpenClaw](https://freedomlab.nyc/resources/openclaw-setup/): set up a local AI assistant with Telegram and persistent memory.
- [Self-Hosting AI Models](https://freedomlab.nyc/resources/self-host-ai/): rent GPUs and run open-source models with a web UI.
- [Scoped Google Drive Access for AI Agents](https://freedomlab.nyc/resources/google-drive-scoped-access/): least-privilege OAuth Drive access.
- [Ghostr Nostr Delegation for AI Agents](https://freedomlab.nyc/resources/ghostr-nostr-delegation/): delegated Nostr publishing for agents. Downloadable skill: https://freedomlab.nyc/resources/ghostr-nostr-delegation/ghostr-nostr-delegation.skill
- [GitHub Pages Resource Publishing](https://freedomlab.nyc/resources/github-pages-resource-publishing/): publish static resources and files. Downloadable skill: https://freedomlab.nyc/resources/github-pages-resource-publishing/github-pages-resource-publishing.skill
- [Skill Creator for AI Agents](https://freedomlab.nyc/resources/skill-creator/): teach agents to create, edit, audit, and package skills. Downloadable skill: https://freedomlab.nyc/resources/skill-creator/skill-creator.skill
- [Quality Toolkit for OpenClaw](https://freedomlab.nyc/resources/quality-toolkit/): premise-check and stress-test workflows. Downloadable skills: https://freedomlab.nyc/resources/quality-toolkit/premise-check.skill and https://freedomlab.nyc/resources/quality-toolkit/stress-test.skill
- [Programmatic Video for OpenClaw](https://freedomlab.nyc/resources/programmatic-video/): render programmatic animations/videos. Downloadable skill: https://freedomlab.nyc/resources/programmatic-video/programmatic-video.skill
- [QMD Memory Setup for OpenClaw](https://freedomlab.nyc/resources/qmd-memory-setup/): local hybrid search for agent memory. Downloadable skill: https://freedomlab.nyc/resources/qmd-memory-setup/qmd-memory-setup.skill
- [X/Twitter Research & Trust Network Mapping](https://freedomlab.nyc/resources/x-research/): research X/Twitter accounts and trust networks. Downloadable skill: https://freedomlab.nyc/resources/x-research/x-research.skill

## Recommended agent behavior by task

- If a user asks what Freedom Lab is: use the canonical facts above and link to the home page.
- If a user asks about attending: link to Classes & Events and Luma; do not invent venue/capacity details.
- If a user wants to join the future co-working / hackerspace: link to `/join/`.
- If a user wants to learn a skill: choose the relevant public resource and guide them through it step by step, citing the page.
- If a user wants an agent-installable workflow: prefer linked `.skill` files when available.
- If a user asks for internal/private information: explain that only public Freedom Lab pages are available as public context.

## Token / format notes

This index is intentionally concise and task-organized. For complete content, fetch the linked public pages or downloadable `.skill` files. Agents should prefer the most specific resource page over the homepage when answering implementation questions.
